verificationNote: Requirements constructed from CSA CCM v4, NIST CSF 2.0, and Malaysia Act 854 obligations. Verify against authoritative sources before use in formal assessments.

domain.name: Key Management
domain.description: Requirements for managing cryptographic keys in cloud environments. Covers cloud KMS usage, key rotation, HSM-backed keys for sensitive workloads, and bring-your-own-key strategies for regulatory compliance.

requirements.title: Cloud KMS for All Encryption Operations
requirements.description: Use cloud-native Key Management Service for all encryption key operations. Centralise key management to ensure consistent access control, audit logging, and lifecycle management. Never store encryption keys in application code, configuration files, or alongside encrypted data.

cspGuidance.aws: Use AWS KMS for all encryption (S3-SSE-KMS, EBS, RDS, Secrets Manager, Lambda environment variables). Create per-application key policies. Enable CloudTrail logging for all KMS API calls.
cspGuidance.azure: Use Azure Key Vault for all key, secret, and certificate management. Configure access policies or RBAC for granular access control. Enable Key Vault diagnostics to Log Analytics.
cspGuidance.gcp: Use Cloud KMS for all CMEK encryption (Cloud Storage, BigQuery, GCE). Configure IAM policies per key ring and key. Enable Data Access audit logs for all KMS operations.

requirements.title: Encryption Key Rotation
requirements.description: Implement automatic key rotation for all encryption keys. Define rotation schedules based on data sensitivity: 90 days for highly sensitive data, 365 days for standard data. Ensure old key versions remain available for decryption of existing data during a defined grace period before disablement.

cspGuidance.aws: Enable automatic key rotation on KMS CMKs (annual rotation with full back-compatibility). Use Secrets Manager rotation Lambda for application secrets with configurable schedules.
cspGuidance.azure: Enable automatic key rotation in Key Vault with configurable rotation period and pre-expiry notification. Use certificate auto-rotation for TLS certificates.
cspGuidance.gcp: Enable automatic key rotation in Cloud KMS with configurable period. Primary key version used for encryption; all versions available for decryption. Use Secret Manager auto-rotation.

requirements.title: HSM-Backed Keys for Sensitive Workloads
requirements.description: Use Hardware Security Module (HSM) backed keys for the most sensitive encryption operations: payment processing, certificate authority operations, regulatory-mandated key protection, and encryption of critical infrastructure data. HSMs provide FIPS 140-2 Level 3 hardware protection.

cspGuidance.aws: Use CloudHSM for dedicated FIPS 140-2 Level 3 HSM clusters. Use KMS Custom Key Store backed by CloudHSM. Standard KMS keys use FIPS 140-2 Level 2 validated HSMs.
cspGuidance.azure: Use Managed HSM for dedicated FIPS 140-2 Level 3 key management. Key Vault Premium tier provides HSM-backed keys. Azure Dedicated HSM for full customer-controlled hardware.
cspGuidance.gcp: Use Cloud KMS with HSM protection level for FIPS 140-2 Level 3 key protection. Cloud External Key Manager for keys held in external HSM infrastructure.

requirements.title: Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) Strategy
requirements.description: Implement BYOK or Hold Your Own Key (HYOK) for workloads requiring customer key custody beyond cloud-managed KMS. BYOK provides key provenance assurance and supports regulatory requirements where keys must not be generated by the CSP. Document key import procedures and split-knowledge key ceremonies.

cspGuidance.aws: Import key material into KMS CMK via key import feature. Use External Key Store (XKS) for keys managed entirely outside AWS. Document key ceremony procedures with split knowledge.
cspGuidance.azure: Import keys into Key Vault or Managed HSM. Use Double Key Encryption for Microsoft 365 data requiring full customer key control. Document key import ceremony.
cspGuidance.gcp: Import key material into Cloud KMS. Use External Key Manager (EKM) for keys held in customer-controlled HSMs (Thales, Fortanix). Enable Key Access Justifications for transparency.
